title: Temporary accommodation arranged for victims of Asla Park fire
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"MOSSEL BAY NEWS -  Following the fire that broke out yesterday, 17 December, in Sixaxeni Street in Asla Park, Mossel Bay Municipality has confirmed that temporary accommodation is being arranged for the families directly impacted.","html":"MOSSEL BAY NEWS - Following the fire that broke out yesterday, 17 December, in Sixaxeni Street in Asla Park, Mossel Bay Municipality has confirmed that temporary accommodation is being arranged for the families directly impacted."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"According to the municipality, more than one home has been affected by the blaze. However, the exact number is yet to be confirmed."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"In collaboration with the municipality, Gift of the Givers arrived in Mossel Bay today, 18 December to distribute emergency aid including blankets, mattresses, food parcels and hygiene packs to affected families.","html":"In collaboration with the municipality, Gift of the Givers arrived in Mossel Bay today, 18 December to distribute emergency aid including blankets, mattresses, food parcels and hygiene packs to affected families."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"\"Following the incident at Asla Park, the municipality responded by utilising its own donation reserves and engaging its strategic partner, Gift of the Givers, whose longstanding partnership with the municipality continues to provide impactful support,\" said the municipality's Director of Corporate Services, Annette Potgieter.","html":"\"Following the incident at Asla Park, the municipality responded by utilising its own donation reserves and engaging its strategic partner, Gift of the Givers, whose longstanding partnership with the municipality continues to provide impactful support,\" said the municipality's Director of Corporate Services, Annette Potgieter."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Mossel Bay Fire, Rescue and Disaster Management Services on the scene of the fire in Alsa Park. Photo: Gabriélla Siebritz","html":"Mossel Bay Fire, Rescue and Disaster Management Services on the scene of the fire in Alsa Park. Photo: Gabriélla Siebritz"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Mossel Bay Fire, Rescue and Disaster Management teams responded to the scene, as well as police and emergency medical services.","html":"Mossel Bay Fire, Rescue and Disaster Management teams responded to the scene, as well as police and emergency medical services."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"The fire, which broke out yesterday morning, was extinguished by 14:30."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Other residents and community members of Asla Park gathered in the street to watch as emergency services tackled the fire, with some filling buckets of water themselves to assist firefighters.","html":"Other residents and community members of Asla Park gathered in the street to watch as emergency services tackled the fire, with some filling buckets of water themselves to assist firefighters."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Also present on the scene was the Garden Route SPCA, which took two animals into its care for safety.","html":"Also present on the scene was the Garden Route SPCA, which took two animals into its care for safety."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The cause of the fire and whether any injuries were sustained is still unclear.","html":"The cause of the fire and whether any injuries were sustained is still unclear."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"If anyone would like to make a contribution to the affected families, contact Gift of the Givers member, Mario Ferreira, via WhatsApp on 082 490 2752.","html":"If anyone would like to make a contribution to the affected families, contact Gift of the Givers member, Mario Ferreira, via WhatsApp on 082 490 2752."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]
